content.thinking: The user is reporting a bug where the developer navigation shows a user ID instead of a name. Let me first look at the attached screenshot to understand the issue, then investigate the codebase.

content.text: Let me look at the screenshot and investigate the issue.

input.prompt:
Find files related to developer navigation in the web app. Look for:
1. Navigation components that display developer names/IDs
2. Routes or pages related to "developers" or "dev" 
3. Any sidebar or navigation that shows a list of developers

The codebase is at /Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1. Focus on apps/web/src/

content.text:
I have a thorough picture now. Here is the organized summary.

---

## File Locations for Developer Navigation in the Web App

### Routing / Entry Point

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/App.tsx` -- Defines the two developer routes at lines 110-111:
  - `developers` renders `DevelopersListPage`
  - `developers/:userId` renders `DeveloperDetailPage`
  - Both are nested under `/dashboard` using `DashboardLayout`

### Layout (Sidebar + Breadcrumb wrapper)

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/layouts/DashboardLayout.tsx` -- Composes `Sidebar`, `Breadcrumb`, and content outlet. Wraps everything in `OrganizationProvider`, `DateRangeProvider`, and `FilterProvider`.

### Sidebar Navigation

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx` -- Contains the `navigation` array (line 42-54) with a "Developers" entry at line 44 pointing to `/dashboard/developers` using the `UserCircle` icon. The sidebar does NOT display a list of individual developer names -- it is a static navigation menu with fixed items (Overview, Developers, Projects, ROI, Sessions, Learnings, Errors).

### Breadcrumb Navigation

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/components/analytics/Breadcrumb.tsx` -- Maps URL segments to labels. The `developers` segment maps to the label "Developers" (line 6). For detail pages like `/dashboard/developers/:userId`, the userId segment falls through to `decodeURIComponent(segment)` at line 22, meaning it displays the raw userId (not a resolved name).

### Developer Pages (where developer names/IDs are displayed)

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/DevelopersListPage.tsx` -- List page showing all developers. Fetches data via `orpc.analytics.developers.list` and `orpc.analytics.developers.trends`. Resolves user IDs to display names using `orpc.analytics.users.mappings` (line 47) and the `formatUsername()` helper. Clicking a row navigates to `/dashboard/developers/${row.user_id}` (line 272).

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/DeveloperDetailPage.tsx` -- Detail page for a single developer. Uses `useParams<{ userId: string }>()` (line 46) to get the userId from the URL. Fetches data via six `orpc.analytics.developers.*` endpoints (details, sessions, projects, timeline, features, errors). Resolves the userId to a display name using `orpc.analytics.users.mappings` and `formatUsername()` (line 113). Contains back-links to `/dashboard/developers` (lines 300, 328).

### Username Resolution (user_id to display name)

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/lib/format.ts` -- Contains `formatUsername(userId, userMap?)` (line 14). Returns the mapped username if found in `userMap`, otherwise returns the raw `userId`.

### Pages That Also Display Developer Names

These pages fetch `orpc.analytics.users.mappings` to resolve user IDs to usernames:

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/SessionsListPage.tsx` (line 84)
- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ROIPage.tsx` (line 67)
- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ProjectDetailPage.tsx` (line 63)
- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/LearningsPage.tsx` (line 48)
- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ErrorsPage.tsx` (line 50)

### Developer-Specific Chart Component

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/components/charts/DeveloperTrendChart.tsx` -- Chart component that splits trend data by developer (uses `user_id` values and accepts a `userMap` prop for name display).

### Filter Context (developer filtering)

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/contexts/FilterContext.tsx` -- Manages `selectedDevelopers` state (line 11). Serializes developer filters to/from URL query params using the `devs` parameter. Provides `setSelectedDevelopers` setter.

### API Contract (shared schemas)

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/packages/api-routes/src/schemas/analytics.ts` -- Defines `DeveloperSummarySchema` (line 93), `DeveloperDetailsSchema` (line 108), `DeveloperSessionSchema` (line 113), `DeveloperDetailsInputSchema` (line 170), `DeveloperSessionsInputSchema` (line 174), and corresponding TypeScript types (lines 518-520).

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/packages/api-routes/src/index.ts` -- Defines the `analytics.developers` RPC contract (line 149) with sub-routes: `list`, `details`, `sessions`, `projects`, `timeline`, `features`, `errors`, `trends`.

### API Server Implementation

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/api/src/handlers/analytics/developers.ts` -- Handler implementing the `developersRouter` with all eight developer endpoints (list, details, sessions, projects, timeline, features, errors, trends).

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/api/src/services/developer.service.ts` -- Service layer with ClickHouse queries for developer data. Queries use `user_id` as the primary identifier and include an optional `username` field (lines 18, 22).

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/api/src/handlers/analytics/index.ts` -- Registers the developers router alongside other analytics routers.

### Hooks

- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/hooks/useAnalyticsQuery.ts` -- Generic analytics query hook used by all developer-related pages.
- `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/hooks/useFullOrganization.ts` -- Organization hook that includes `members` data (line 9), used in the DevelopersListPage for member count.

### Key Observation About Developer Name Display

The sidebar does not list individual developers. Developer names/IDs appear in two places:

1. **DevelopersListPage** -- a table listing all developers with clickable rows
2. **Breadcrumb** -- when on a developer detail page, the breadcrumb shows the raw `userId` segment (not the resolved username), since `Breadcrumb.tsx` has no access to `userMappings`

content.thinking:
I can see the issue clearly now. The breadcrumb shows the raw user ID (`REDACTED`) instead of the developer's name. The problem is in the `Breadcrumb.tsx` component - it doesn't have access to user mappings and just displays the raw URL segment.

Let me read the relevant files to understand the current implementation and fix it.

content.text:

Let me read the relevant files.

49→ const chartTheme = useChartTheme(); 50→ const days = calculateDays(); 51→ 52→ const [projectFilter, setProjectFilter] = useState(""); 53→ const [outcomeFilter, setOutcomeFilter] = useState<"all" | "success">("all"); 54→ 55→ const { data: details, isLoading: detailsLoading } = useAnalyticsQuery( 56→ orpc.analytics.developers.details.queryOptions({ 57→ input: { userId: userId as string, days }, 58→ }), 59→ ); 60→ 61→ const { data: sessions } = useAnalyticsQuery( 62→ orpc.analytics.developers.sessions.queryOptions({ 63→ input: { 64→ userId: userId as string, 65→ days, 66→ limit: 100, 67→ projectPath: projectFilter || undefined, 68→ outcome: outcomeFilter, 69→ sortBy: "date" as const, 70→ sortOrder: "desc" as const, 71→ }, 72→ }), 73→ ); content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue. The `Breadcrumb.tsx` component doesn't have access to user mappings, so when viewing `/dashboard/developers/:userId`, it just shows the raw user ID in the breadcrumb.

The fix is to make the Breadcrumb component aware of user mappings so it can resolve user IDs to display names. The `DeveloperDetailPage` already fetches user mappings and resolves the username - we need to bring similar logic into the Breadcrumb.

The cleanest approach would be to:
1. Have the Breadcrumb detect when a segment follows "developers" in the path
2. Fetch user mappings and resolve the ID to a name


3. Leverage React Query's caching so the fetch from DeveloperDetailPage means the Breadcrumb just hits the cache

I should also check how project names are handled in breadcrumbs since they follow the same pattern with path-based detail pages.

content.text:
Now I see the problem. The `Breadcrumb.tsx` has no awareness of user mappings — when the URL is `/dashboard/developers/:userId`, it just shows the raw `userId` segment. The `DeveloperDetailPage` already resolves the name via `orpc.analytics.users.mappings`, so the fix is to add the same resolution to the breadcrumb.

Since the detail page already fetches user mappings (and React Query caches them), adding the same query to the Breadcrumb will just hit the cache — no extra network request.

content.content: The file /Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/components/analytics/Breadcrumb.tsx has been updated successfully.

content.text: Now let me check if `useAnalyticsQuery` supports an `enabled` option — I need to verify the hook signature.

content.thinking:
The `useAnalyticsQuery` hook takes `UseQueryOptions<TData>` and doesn't accept a second argument. The `enabled` option is part of `UseQueryOptions`, so I need to pass it inside the options object, not as a separate argument.

Let me fix the Breadcrumb to pass `enabled` correctly.

content.text: The `useAnalyticsQuery` hook only takes a single `options` object — `enabled` needs to be inside it, not as a second argument. Let me fix that.

content.content: The file /Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/tunis-v1/apps/web/src/components/analytics/Breadcrumb.tsx has been updated successfully.

content.text: Let me verify the final file looks correct.
Now let me run the type checker to make sure everything compiles.

Type checks pass. Here's a summary of the fix:

**Problem:** The `Breadcrumb` component at `apps/web/src/components/analytics/Breadcrumb.tsx` displayed raw user IDs (like `REDACTED`) in the breadcrumb when navigating to `/dashboard/developers/:userId`.

**Fix:** The breadcrumb now:
1. Detects when the path contains a developer detail page (a segment after `developers`)
2. Fetches user mappings via `orpc.analytics.users.mappings` (only when needed, using `enabled: hasDeveloperDetail`)
3. Resolves the user ID to a display name using the existing `formatUsername()` helper
4. Since `DeveloperDetailPage` already fetches the same data, React Query serves it from cache — no extra network request

The breadcrumb will now show `Overview > Developers > John Smith` instead of `Overview > Developers > REDACTED`.

navigating to a developer detail page. Now uses useUserMap hook to
resolve IDs to display names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
